headline: New war in Lebanon is price of international community's silence
subhead: Ever since Israel and Lebanon signed a theoretical truce in November 2024, which Israel never respected, the international community has stayed almost entirely silent, implicitly accepting the rule of force over international law.

headline: US temporarily allows sale of some Russian oil
subhead: As the war in the Middle East upends the world's energy and transport sectors, the United States announced the authorization of the delivery and sale of Russian crude oil and petroleum products at sea, marking a temporary easing of economic sanctions against Russia over its invasion of Ukraine.

headline: Hong Kong's complex role in bypassing sanctions against Russia
subhead: According to a report by a US-based NGO published on February 18, Hong Kong is enabling European products banned from export to Russia to circumvent allied sanctions.

headline: Pritzker Prize honors Chilean Smiljan Radic, architect of interiority
subhead: Ten years after Alejandro Aravena, Radic has become the second Chilean to be awarded what is considered the Nobel Prize of architecture.

headline: In this northern French town, far-right voters are no longer an 'anomaly'
subhead: Doubt has taken root in Lens ahead of the March 15 and 22 municipal elections. A century-old Socialist stronghold, the town with a mining past could fall to the far-right Rassemblement National.

Jeffrey Epstein's Parisian lives
subhead: Over 18 years, the extremely kitsch property at 22 Avenue Foch served Epstein as a European office, a salon for artists and scientists and, above all, one of the places where he committed his sexual crimes.

headline: The ghosts of the new Chilean president's family
subhead: José Antonio Kast, who will be sworn in on Wednesday, makes no secret of his admiration for Augusto Pinochet's regime. He is less forthcoming about his father's past as a former Wehrmacht soldier and his family's role during the atrocities committed in Paine during the 1973 coup.

headline: One week before France's local elections, electorate leans right, poll shows
subhead: With environmental issues taking a back seat, security issues coming to the fore and a clear increase in support for the far-right Rassemblement National, the survey conducted by Ipsos BVA for Le Monde, Cevipof and the Jean Jaurès Foundation paints an unprecedented picture of the French political landscape.
